review_title: Harvest: An Excellent Resource For Tracking Work Time
description: Overall, I really love the product. It is easy to use and convenient to manage time for tasks and projects.
author: Luke E.
pros: Harvest is a great tool for time tracking - especially for organizations where most/all workers are remote.
cons: Sometimes, it can be difficult to remember to stop or start the timer. It would help if Harvest had better reminders. Harvest does have some type of reminder if it perceives the clock has run too long. But it doesn't always remind the user until it is way too late.

review_title: Easy, customizable, and simple time tracking
author: Stephanie L.
pros: Very customizable with the time sheet options and the ability to add categories and sub categories to a specific time block. Easy to learn right away with a simple and clean user interface and able to easily edit time so it is not rigid or if you accidentally forget it on you can go back and fix it.
cons: I don't like that it does not send you the "did you leave your timer on" towards the end of my work day as a reminder to turn it off. Instead I get it at odd hours or the next morning and I have to figure out when I actually stopped working because the clock just keeps going.

review_title: Simple tool to get started but lacks potential to scale
description: Its a simple product that can be quickly setup and used for simple time tracking and invoice generation.
author: Max W.
pros: Ease of use. Theres basically nothing you can do wrong.
cons: The small amount of functionalities and the lack of customization possibilities.

review_title: Great for managing multiple clients
author: Olivia R.
pros: Harvest is excellent for managing my time tracking across multiple clients, and multiple projects for each of those clients. Having worked in a previous organization that required time tracking, yet didn’t have a tool implemented, it was so cumbersome to maintain my time on a spreadsheet. I also appreciate the ability to make changes—if I forget to switch my timer between clients, or if I attend an event not in front of my computer (where I didn’t start a time on my phone).
cons: Nothing—gets the job done well for my use case! It could be nice if using the PTO code would sync with PTO tracking in our HRIS, but it’s possible that’s a setting our team hadn’t enabled.

review_title: Saving time with Harvest
description: Overall a solid platform as long as you have a normal work pattern and does save time on the expenses side.
author: Mike S.
pros: From a user perspective it is easy to use and manage and the expense capture from the phone app makes it very easy to capture as soon as you get a receipt in your hand.
cons: The calendar is set to a standard working week (Mon to Fri) however when with clients in Saudi Arabia the working week is Sunday to Thursday so a week is split over 2 time sheets and can't be adjusted. Does make it a little manual when creating client invoices and timesheet matching.

review_title: Harvest Review
description: Detailed reporting and analytics: Generate insights on time spent, team productivity, budgets, and profitability to inform decisions.
author: Junior T.
pros: Expense tracking and team management: Log expenses, approve timesheets, and monitor billable hours with automated reminders.
cons: Limited offline access: Requires internet for most functions, posing issues in low-connectivity areas.

review_title: Time tracking made easy!
description: Very positive, very easy to understand and we all used it for a small company for a long time, as we grew it was harder to use.
author: Harry D.
pros: The most simple time tracking software i've ever used! Easy to understand and very good at time tracking and project timesheets.
cons: Unfortunately there's other products out there that offer multiple additional features, Harvest is just time tracking whereas others offer billing etc.

review_title: Harvest your time
description: Overall I really enjoy Harvest and it has always helped keep my teams on track.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: No matter the size of your team, Harvest makes it easy to track time to individual roles and projects. Budget tracking is also extremely easy for Project Managers. Exporting reports is quick and simple.
cons: Harvest is pretty straightforward and easy to use, but I wish there were more ways to customize invoices and reports—it feels a bit limiting. Also, it doesn’t have features like GPS tracking or more detailed employee management tools.

review_title: Good project time tracking software platform
description: I used Harvest in past for one of my client with its integration with Asana and liked its simple interface. For a newbie, it is a good time tracking program but still need more advanced functionality.
author: Ankit D.
pros: The platform is simple and accurate. It offers integration with many other project management tools including Asana and Google Calender. Setting up time tracking for individual projects is easy and flexible. You can anytime edit start and end date for any project without any issues. Project invoice generation is flawless and easy.
cons: It doesn't offer automatic time tracking and we have to manually enter the values for each project. They offer limited features for free plan which I don't think is a big limitation. It doesn't work offline and require a stable internet connection for accurate time tracking.

review_title: Easy To Implement And Simple Time Tracking
description: We needed a time management system for all of our team to use and have data synced for billable hours to clients. Harvest was easy to set up and install and get everyone on the same page in about an hour.
author: David K.
pros: Harvest is super easy to set up and start using right away for accurate time tracking on projects and work.
cons: Costs, I know they need to make money but for time tracking in 2025 - it's not something that businesses want to pay more and more for something that's standard unless there are more value added with other features.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Timetracking and Invoicing Made Easy
description: In general, I have been very happy with this product. When we originally were researching time tracking and invoicing software this one rose to the surface and it has remained one of the leaders in this space. Having said that, it's not cheap, particularly if you need multiple people on it, so there are definitely options out there that cost less and or are free if that is what you are looking for. It does also provide some integration with Quickbooks that others likely don't have.
author: Will T.
pros: Working in a creative field, I am a person who detests tracking time and invoicing. It takes me out of flow and into a space that doesn't really foster creative ideas or spark the imagination. Harvest makes the process about as painless as possible (let's be real, tracking time will never be entirely painless) and frees me up to focus on what I do and how I best deliver value to my clients and team. For clients that prefer to pay by credit card, it allows you to deliver the invoice via a web portal with the ability to pay via PayPal with your cc.
cons: I still feel that the project setup scenarios could be simplified and / or streamlined. I've used Harvest for over 10 years and they have consistently added features and addressed certain gray areas that the software couldn't really account for, but I do think that if they were rebuilding the platform knowing what they know now, they would make it even more straightforward and simple from that standpoint.

review_title: Harvest is a Freelancer's more affordable time-tracking and invoicing option
description: Harvest has been a game changer for me. When I started my business I was easily able to track hours, expenses, and bill clients without paying outrageous fees for software that had more than I actually needed (and was challenging to learn).
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The best part about this software--the price. There is a free option for new freelancers, and for my first year, I strictly used that. Even the free version is highly robust. However, I quickly realized I needed something a bit more custom, and I've upgraded to the paid version, but even that is very reasonable.
cons: I don't really like that you can't send contracts. I feel like if the program includes estimates, it should also have a contract-sending option. I understand it integrates with other contract platforms, but those were so very expensive-—or just didn't appeal to me.

review_title: Efficient Time and Expense Tracking with Harvest Software
description: I have used Harvest software for project management and time tracking purposes. One of the key features that I found useful is the time tracking function, which allowed me to track the time spent on tasks, projects, and clients. This helped me to manage my time more efficiently and accurately bill my clients.In addition, I have also used the project management feature, which enabled me to create tasks, assign them to team members, set due dates, and track progress. This helped me to stay on top of my projects and ensure that tasks were completed on time.I have also used the invoicing feature, which allowed me to create and send invoices to clients based on the time spent on tasks. The software also provided the option to set up recurring invoices and track payment status, which made invoicing more efficient and streamlined.
author: Andualem W.
pros: 1.Time tracking: Harvest is particularly effective at tracking time for individuals and teams, providing users with detailed reports that help them manage their time more effectively.2.Project management: Harvest's project management features are powerful and user-friendly, allowing users to easily collaborate and manage tasks across multiple projects.3.Invoicing and payments: Harvest streamlines the invoicing process, making it easy to send invoices to clients and track payments.4.Integration: Harvest integrates with a wide range of other software and tools, including accounting and CRM systems, which can help businesses manage their operations more efficiently.
cons: 1.Pricing: Harvest can be expensive for businesses that need to use multiple features or have a larger team.2.Limited customization: Some users may find that Harvest's features are too rigid and don't allow for enough customization to meet their specific needs.3.Reporting limitations: While Harvest provides detailed reports, some users may find that the reporting functionality is limited in certain areas.

review_title: Harvest is a Perfect Solution for Time Tracking and Billing
description: Everyone in the agency from me, the owner, to all of our team members love using Harvest. It's so easy to use, between the desktop version to the mobile app. It's been a lifesaver to make keeping track of time and budgets for our marketing agency.
author: Kurt H.
pros: I started my marketing agency in 1999. I used manual, paper solutions for time tracking for the first few years. After much research we found Workamajig in 2004 to switch to an online solution. It served its purpose for many years but everyone struggled with it and found it not user friendly at all. After many attempts we found Harvest in 2018. I played with it for a few days at the end of a month then ripped the bandaid and switched to it on the 1st of a month. It was SO user-friendly everyone loved it out the gate and we have never looked back. It's so great for time tracking and quick insight into projects and budget status at the client, project and task level. I love it!
cons: It's very hard to complain about Harvest. I really love it. I do wish it had the ability to set budgets for staff by clients/projects so we can see how booked up our team is and better manage their time allocation. There is a second-party project Harvest syncs with to try to do this, but it does not work the way we needed it to so we dropped it after a brief trial.

review_title: Harvest is the best choice for Small Business Owners
description: My overall experience with Harvest has been outstanding. I would recommend it to anyone looking for a simple, straightforward platform to do their invoicing and time tracking.
author: Mathew K.
pros: What I really like most about the Harvest platform is the ease of use. I have used it for years and it has always been 100% reliable! It is easy to add new clients, set up recurring invoices, and keep track of my projects.
cons: It would be great if you could handle setting up invoices from their mobile app but this isn't a deal-breaker. I travel often so that would just be nice to have. That said, I can still log in to the desktop site while traveling and do whatever I need.

review_title: Easy Time Tracking
description: I have been using Harvest to organize my working h/o, and it can be the most basic and efficient tool for freelancers
author: Kimb W.
pros: Harvest is a fantastic way of tracking time as it only takes a split second to record to deduct hours
cons: The only con I could mention is that the free plan might not satisfy you when you work on more than one project at a time

review_title: Easy to use for individual time tracking and project time tracking.
description: Overall it's been a good experience. I've been using it for the past 1 1/2 years to manage my own time and also track project time for clients.
author: Allison S.
pros: I like that it's fairly intuitive. The user interface is clean and friendly. It integrates well with Google Chrome. I find it easy to set up projects and track time.
cons: I wish on an individual time card you could see what time (clock time) was logged instead of total time. I.e. see that 10 minutes was logged from 9:00-9:10 instead of just 10 minutes total.

review_title: Harvesting you time correctly
description: Harvest is great. It helps my Job keep track of multiple clients and what I do for them. Makes it easy to break down each task and even comment about the task
author: Bambi S.
pros: I like how if you have to manually enter the number of minutes into the system and it converts the time to the decimal percentage for you. Harvest doesn't make you have to stop and figure it out yourself
cons: Very touchy. If you accidentally hit enter or esc you must start all over

review_title: The interface looks a bit dated
description: Harvest is a great choice for freelance graphic designers looking for a simple and effective tool to manage their time and billing
author: Maysa C.
pros: Intuitive interface for tracking time on each project, eh? Powerful tools to generate professional invoices, and the price is right, bud
cons: The interface could be a bit more modern, hey? And some advanced features require a paid subscription

review_title: Great Platform for Self-Employed
description: Overall, Harvest has been great! I’m glad I found it for tracking my contracted time.
author: Sammantha B.
pros: I love the invoicing option within Harvest.
cons: Wish I could name projects the same name just with different codes

review_title: High quality timesheet tool
author: Edward K.
pros: Quality. The UI is very slick; they've really gone deep on how people use the product. Things like keyboard shortcuts and supporting regular vs decimal time when entering timesheets are a real time saver.
cons: Some of the month-by-month project reporting requires paging through each month. It would be nice to see several months in one view.

review_title: Easy to Use!
description: Overall, I enjoy the platform for how efficient and easy to use it is.
author: Alyssa N.
pros: It is easy to use and user friendly, even to those who have never used the platform.
cons: There is currently no way to link tasks to specific user roles, however that is not a major issue.

review_title: Excels at time tracking and expenses, don't look for more.
author: Roy C.
pros: Does exactly what it says it can do, track time and it does it well.
cons: Only does one thing for the monthly price that adds up per seat.

review_title: Efficient and well-integrated time-tracker tool
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The best thing about Harvest for me is how it well it can be integrated with other tools, so you can log your time from anywhere.
cons: The only thing I don’t really like about Harvest is that it’s quite pricey.

review_title: Great for PM, so-so for wage and hour compliance
description: A great piece of software for project management for sure.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The best thing about Harvest is how easy it is for employees to use. It took a little to no training to roll this out.
cons: Overall my biggest gripe with the software is that for some reason it doesn’t track overtime or meal break penalties would still requires our payroll person to go in and calculate all of those manually.
